hero.alt: Ring-tailed lemur sitting upright showing its black-and-white striped tail
hero.caption: Ring-tailed lemurs are easy to spot thanks to their long, black-and-white ringed tails.

habitat.alt: Ring-tailed lemur perched on a branch in a leafy tree
habitat.caption: Ring-tailed lemurs live only on Madagascar, in forests and spiny scrub in the south.

diet.fileTitle: File:Ring-tailed Lemur (Lemur catta) feeding on tamarind leaves, Tsimanampetsotsa, Madagascar (4309672928).jpg
diet.alt: Ring-tailed lemur feeding on tamarind leaves in a Madagascar forest
diet.caption: Lemurs are omnivores, but they mostly eat fruit and leaves, especially from tamarind trees.

baby.fileTitle: File:Ringtail Lemur with baby on her back (5396260658).jpg
baby.alt: Baby ring-tailed lemur riding on its mother's back
baby.caption: A lemur baby clings to its mother's belly, then rides on her back as it grows bigger.

family.alt: Group of ring-tailed lemurs resting close together
family.caption: Ring-tailed lemurs live in big groups called troops, often led by a top female.

range.alt: Small group of ring-tailed lemurs walking on the ground at Berenty, Madagascar
range.caption: Lemurs spend much of their time on the ground, traveling across their forest home in southern Madagascar.

size.alt: Full body view of a ring-tailed lemur with its long tail curled up
size.caption: A lemur's body is about the size of a house cat, but its tail is even longer than its body.

closeup.alt: Close-up of a ring-tailed lemur's face with bright orange eyes
closeup.caption: Big orange eyes and a black-and-white face help lemurs see and recognize each other.

fun-fact.alt: Ring-tailed lemurs sitting up with arms out, sunbathing
fun-fact.caption: On cool mornings lemurs sit up and spread their arms to soak up the sun like little sunbathers.

core-habitat.alt: Ring-tailed lemur climbing among trees in a Madagascar reserve
core-habitat.caption: Forests give lemurs trees to climb, fruit to eat, and safe places to sleep at night.

core-diet.fileTitle: File:Ring-tailed Lemur (Lemur catta) feeding on tamarind leaves, Tsimanampetsotsa, Madagascar (4309671260).jpg
core-diet.alt: Ring-tailed lemur reaching for tamarind leaves to eat
core-diet.caption: Lemurs use their hands to grab leaves, fruit, and flowers, and they also drink nectar.

core-baby.alt: Mother ring-tailed lemur sitting with her young baby
core-baby.caption: Lemur mothers care for their babies closely, and other troop members help watch them too.

core-family.caption: Living in a troop helps lemurs stay safe, share warmth, and watch out for danger.

core-range.alt: Ring-tailed lemur standing alert in its habitat
core-range.caption: Ring-tailed lemurs are found only in southern and southwestern Madagascar, nowhere else on Earth.
